text:
Introduction

CodelistGenerator is a package designed to explore the vocabulary of a database, and create codelists.
The code is publicly available in DARWIN-EU’s GitHub repository CodelistGenerator.
CohortGenerator v4.0.2 is available in CRAN
For further details about the functionalities of the package, see the website

Outline:

Explore the OMOP vocabularies available in your database:


Exploring the vocabulary tables



Finding codes: creating a codelist




Generate a candidate codelist
Generating codelists for medications
Manipulate codelists


Assessing the use of the codes of your codelist: CodelistDiagnostics

section: Explore OMOP vocabularies in your database
text: Explore OMOP vocabularies in your database

1.1. Exploring the vocabularies

Create a mock database:



library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(dplyr)
library(CDMConnector)
library(CodelistGenerator)

requireEunomia()


Download completed!

# Connect to the database and create the cdm object
con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), 
                 eunomiaDir("synpuf-1k", "5.3"))


Download completed!

cdm <- cdmFromCon(con = con, 
                  cdmName = "Eunomia Synpuf",
                  cdmSchema   = "main",
                  writeSchema = "main", 
                  achillesSchema = "main")

1.1. Exploring the vocabularies

Access different tables using the $



cdm$person

# A query:  ?? x 18
# Database: DuckDB 1.5.4 [unknown@Linux 6.17.0-1018-azure:R 4.4.1//tmp/RtmpyzaYgm/file1a875199a7a6.duckdb]

       <int>             <int>         <int>          <int>        <int>
 1         0              8507          1932              7            1
 2         1              8532          1957              9            1
 3         2              8532          1934             11            1
 4         3              8507          1942              9            1
 5         4              8507          1934              7            1

 7         6              8532          1942              5            1
 8         7              8507          1943              4            1
 9         8              8532          1951             12            1
10         9              8532          1942              2            1

#   race_source_concept_id <int>, ethnicity_source_value <chr>,
#   ethnicity_source_concept_id <int>

1.1. Exploring the vocabularies

The cdm contains different vocabulary tables we can work with.
This tables contain the information that we can find in ATHENA



# All the concepts: 
cdm$concept

# All the relationships:
cdm$concept_relationship

# All the vertical relationships:
cdm$concept_ancestor

# All the synonym relationships:
cdm$concept_synonym

# All drug-related data:
cdm$drug_strength

1.1. Exploring the vocabularies

Let’s see the example of concept_name = "acetaminophen"

cdm$concept |> filter(concept_id == 1125315)

1.1. Exploring the vocabularies

How can we use CodelistGenerator to explore all these vocabularies?
Vocabulary version:



vocabularyVersion(cdm)

[1] "v5.0 06-AUG-21"

1.1. Exploring the vocabularies

Explore the package website to see more available* family functions:










If you want us to add more available available* family functions… open an issue!
Finding codes: creating a codelist

2.0. What is a codelist?

A codelist is an object of class list() that contains the codes to define a specific conditions/drug/measurement…
We have three different ways to define a codelist in CodelistGenerator:
A codelist



codelist <- list("codes1" = c(1L, 2L, 3L),
                 "codes2" = c(4L, 5L, 10L))
codelist <- newCodelist(codelist)











codelist$codes1

[1] 1 2 3





class(codelist)

[1] "codelist" "list"    





names(codelist)

[1] "codes1" "codes2"

2.0. What is a codelist?

A codelist_with_details



codelist_with_details <- list(
  "codes1" = tibble("concept_id" = c(1L, 2L, 3L),
                    "concept_name" = c("Musculoskeletal disorder", "Osteoarthrosis", "Arthritis"),
                    "domain_id" = c("Condition", "Condition", "Condition"),
                    "vocabulary_id" = c("SNOMED","SNOMED","SNOMED"),
                    "standard_concept" = c("S","S","S")),
  "codes2" = tibble("concept_id" = c(4L, 5L, 10L),
                    "concept_name" = c("Osteoarthritis of knee", "Osteoarthritis of hip", "Adalimumab"),
                    "domain_id" = c("Condition", "Condition", "Drug"),
                    "vocabulary_id" = c("SNOMED","SNOMED","RxNorm"),
                    "standard_concept" = c("S","S","S")))
codelist_with_details <- newCodelistWithDetails(codelist_with_details)











codelist_with_details$codes1

# A tibble: 3 × 5
  concept_id concept_name             domain_id vocabulary_id standard_concept
       <int> <chr>                    <chr>     <chr>         <chr>           
1          1 Musculoskeletal disorder Condition SNOMED        S               
2          2 Osteoarthrosis           Condition SNOMED        S               
3          3 Arthritis                Condition SNOMED        S               





class(codelist_with_details)

[1] "codelist_with_details" "list"                 





names(codelist_with_details)

[1] "codes1" "codes2"

2.0. What is a codelist?

A concept_set_expression



concept_set_expression <- list(
  "codes1" = tibble("concept_id" = c(1L, 2L, 3L),
                    "excluded" = c(FALSE, FALSE, FALSE),
                    "descendants" = c(TRUE, FALSE, FALSE),
                    "mapped" = c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE)),
  "codes2" = tibble("concept_id" = c(4L, 5L, 10L),
                    "excluded" = c(FALSE, FALSE, FALSE),
                    "descendants" = c(FALSE, FALSE, FALSE),
                    "mapped" = c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE))
)


concept_set_expression <- newConceptSetExpression(concept_set_expression)











concept_set_expression$codes1

# A tibble: 3 × 4
  concept_id excluded descendants mapped
       <int> <lgl>    <lgl>       <lgl> 
1          1 FALSE    TRUE        TRUE  
2          2 FALSE    FALSE       TRUE  
3          3 FALSE    FALSE       TRUE  





class(concept_set_expression)

[1] "concept_set_expression" "conceptSetExpression"   "list"                  





names(concept_set_expression)

[1] "codes1" "codes2"

2.0.1 Why are codelist important?

Workflow:

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

Instead of creating the codelist manually, we can use CodelistGenerator to generate it for us:



candidate_codes <- getCandidateCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  keywords = "Musculoskeletal disorder",
  domains = NULL, 
  standardConcept = "Standard",
  includeDescendants = TRUE,
  searchInSynonyms = TRUE,
  searchNonStandard = FALSE,
  includeAncestor = FALSE
)

candidate_codes

# A tibble: 12,859 × 6
   concept_id found_from   concept_name domain_id vocabulary_id standard_concept
        <int> <chr>        <chr>        <chr>     <chr>         <chr>           
 1      30234 From descen… Neck sprain  Condition SNOMED        S               
 2      72398 From descen… Arthropathy… Condition SNOMED        S               
 3      72399 From descen… Arthropathy… Condition SNOMED        S               
 4      72400 From descen… Arthropathy… Condition SNOMED        S               
 5      72401 From descen… Kashin-Bek … Condition SNOMED        S               
 6      72403 From descen… Hemarthrosi… Condition SNOMED        S               
 7      72405 From descen… Degenerativ… Condition SNOMED        S               
 8      72406 From descen… Old disrupt… Condition SNOMED        S               
 9      72407 From descen… Effusion of… Condition SNOMED        S               
10      72410 From descen… Infection o… Condition SNOMED        S               
# ℹ 12,849 more rows

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

Instead of creating the codelist manually, we can use CodelistGenerator to generate it for us:



candidate_codes |> distinct(found_from)

# A tibble: 3 × 1
  found_from         
  <chr>              
1 From descendants   
2 From initial search
3 In synonyms

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

You can restrict to only ‘domain = “Condition”’ for a more accurate search:



candidate_codes <- getCandidateCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  keywords = "Musculoskeletal disorder",
  domains = "Condition", 
  standardConcept = "Standard",
  includeDescendants = TRUE,
  searchInSynonyms = TRUE,
  searchNonStandard = FALSE,
  includeAncestor = FALSE
)

candidate_codes

# A tibble: 12,798 × 6
   concept_id found_from   concept_name domain_id vocabulary_id standard_concept
        <int> <chr>        <chr>        <chr>     <chr>         <chr>           
 1      30234 From descen… Neck sprain  Condition SNOMED        S               
 2      72398 From descen… Arthropathy… Condition SNOMED        S               
 3      72399 From descen… Arthropathy… Condition SNOMED        S               
 4      72400 From descen… Arthropathy… Condition SNOMED        S               
 5      72401 From descen… Kashin-Bek … Condition SNOMED        S               
 6      72403 From descen… Hemarthrosi… Condition SNOMED        S               
 7      72405 From descen… Degenerativ… Condition SNOMED        S               
 8      72406 From descen… Old disrupt… Condition SNOMED        S               
 9      72407 From descen… Effusion of… Condition SNOMED        S               
10      72410 From descen… Infection o… Condition SNOMED        S               
# ℹ 12,788 more rows

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

We can exclude concepts with specific words



candidate_codes_excl <- getCandidateCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  keywords = "Musculoskeletal disorder",
  domains = "Condition", 
  exclude = "congenital",
  standardConcept = "Standard",
  includeDescendants = TRUE,
  searchInSynonyms = TRUE,
  searchNonStandard = FALSE,
  includeAncestor = FALSE
)




# Compare codelists:
res <- compareCodelists(candidate_codes, candidate_codes_excl)
res

# A tibble: 12,798 × 3
   concept_id concept_name                                              codelist
        <int> <chr>                                                     <chr>   
 1      30234 Neck sprain                                               Both    
 2      72398 Arthropathy in Behcet's syndrome                          Both    
 3      72399 Arthropathy in Behcet's syndrome of multiple sites        Both    
 4      72400 Arthropathy of the pelvic region and thigh associated wi… Both    
 5      72401 Kashin-Bek disease                                        Both    
 6      72403 Hemarthrosis of the ankle and/or foot                     Both    
 7      72405 Degenerative joint disease of shoulder region             Both    
 8      72406 Old disruption of ligament of knee other than collateral… Both    
 9      72407 Effusion of joint of shoulder region                      Both    
10      72410 Infection of bone                                         Both    
# ℹ 12,788 more rows

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

We can exclude concepts with specific words



res |>
  filter(codelist == "Only in codelist codelist1")

# A tibble: 660 × 3
   concept_id concept_name                                        codelist      
        <int> <chr>                                               <chr>         
 1      73021 Bilateral congenital dislocation of hip             Only in codel…
 2      73872 Congenital deformity of knee joint                  Only in codel…
 3      74150 Congenital subluxation of hip, bilateral            Only in codel…
 4      74468 Congenital anomaly of rib                           Only in codel…
 5      74471 Congenital coxa valga                               Only in codel…
 6      75073 Congenital dislocation of knee with genu recurvatum Only in codel…
 7      75356 Congenital anomaly of sternocleidomastoid muscle    Only in codel…
 8      75804 Congenital myotonic dystrophy                       Only in codel…
 9      75915 Congenital absence of vertebra                      Only in codel…
10      77373 Congenital deformity of hip joint                   Only in codel…
# ℹ 650 more rows

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

getCandidateCodes() output is a candidate_codes object:



class(candidate_codes_excl)

[1] "candidate_codes" "tbl_df"          "tbl"             "data.frame"     

searchStrategy(candidate_codes_excl)

# A tibble: 1 × 10
  cdm_name       vocabulary_version keywords    exclude domains standard_concept
  <chr>          <chr>              <chr>       <chr>   <chr>   <chr>           
1 Eunomia Synpuf v5.0 06-AUG-21     "\"Musculo… "\"con… "\"con… "\"Standard\""  
# ℹ 4 more variables: search_in_synonyms <lgl>, search_non_standard <lgl>,
#   include_descendants <lgl>, include_ancestor <lgl>

2.1. Your turn!

Let’s do a small exercice to practice creating codelists!
First, create your CDM object:



library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(dplyr)
library(CDMConnector)
library(CodelistGenerator)

requireEunomia()

# Connect to the database and create the cdm object
con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), 
                 eunomiaDir("synpuf-1k", "5.3"))
cdm <- cdmFromCon(con = con, 
                  cdmName = "Eunomia Synpuf",
                  cdmSchema   = "main",
                  writeSchema = "main", 
                  achillesSchema = "main")

2.1. Your turn!

Create your candidate “diabetes” codelist using getCandidateCodes() excluding the word pregnancy, excluding synonyms, including descendants, excluding ancestors, not searching “non-standard” codes, and not restricting to any domain in particular. You can use the command ??getCandidateCodes in case you need more information about specific inputs.
Transform the candidate_codes object to a codelist using asCodelist()
Export your codelist using exportCodelist()



Don’t go to the next slide! There are the results there…

2.1. Solution

diabetes <- getCandidateCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  keywords = "diabetes",
  domains = NULL, 
  exclude = "pregnancy",
  standardConcept = "Standard",
  includeDescendants = TRUE,
  searchInSynonyms = FALSE,
  searchNonStandard = FALSE,
  includeAncestor = FALSE
)

codelist <- asCodelist(diabetes)

exportCodelist(codelist, path = here(), type = "csv")

importCodelist(path = here(), type = "csv")

# Similar functions for the other types of codelists:
asCodelistWithDetails(diabetes)
asConceptSetExpression(diabetes)

exportConceptSetExpression(...)
# in the future: exportCodelistWithDetails()

importConceptSetExpression(...)

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

Careful with the use of exclusion!



c1 <- getCandidateCodes(
    cdm = cdm,
    keywords = "Depression",
    exclude = NULL,
    includeDescendants = FALSE
)

c2 <- getCandidateCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  keywords = "Depression",
  exclude = c("ST depression"),
  includeDescendants = FALSE
)

res <- compareCodelists(c1, c2)
res |>
  filter(codelist == "Only in codelist codelist1")

# A tibble: 27 × 3
   concept_id concept_name                                     codelist         
        <int> <chr>                                            <chr>            
 1    4095285 ST segment depression                            Only in codelist…
 2    4102973 Postviral depression                             Only in codelist…
 3    4108454 Single stimulus depression - finding             Only in codelist…
 4    4129184 Severe postnatal depression                      Only in codelist…
 5    4129842 Mild postnatal depression                        Only in codelist…
 6    4141225 Postoperative supratip depression of nose        Only in codelist…
 7    4161569 Depression of left ventricular systolic function Only in codelist…
 8    4167563 Central nervous system depression                Only in codelist…
 9    4171066 Drug-induced central nervous system depression   Only in codelist…
10    4197222 Stuporous depression                             Only in codelist…
# ℹ 17 more rows

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

Exclude partial match (without accounting for word boundaries): “/ST depression/”
Exclude exact match: “/\bST depression/\b”



getCandidateCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  keywords = "Depression",
  exclude = c("/ST depression/"),
  includeDescendants = FALSE
)
# This WOULD NOT exclude "ST segmental depression" but WOULD exclude "poST depression"

getCandidateCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  keywords = "Depression",
  exclude = c("/\bST depression/\b"),
  includeDescendants = FALSE
)
# This WOULD NOT exclude neither "ST segmental depression" or "poST depression", but WOULD exclude "prior ST depression"


See the vignette for a more detailed example

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

If you don’t want a candidate codelist, but you have identified a concept and you just want to get all its decendants, you can use getDescendants()
See an example for the concept Senile dementia with depression



getDescendants(cdm, 
               conceptId = 379784)

# A tibble: 2 × 10
  concept_id concept_name               domain_id vocabulary_id concept_class_id
       <int> <chr>                      <chr>     <chr>         <chr>           
1    4097384 Primary degenerative deme… Condition SNOMED        Clinical Finding
2     379784 Senile dementia with depr… Condition SNOMED        Clinical Finding
# ℹ 5 more variables: standard_concept <chr>, concept_code <chr>,
#   valid_start_date <date>, valid_end_date <date>, invalid_reason <chr>

2.1. Generate a candidate codelist

Check which ingredients are available in your database:



adi <- availableDrugIngredients(cdm)
adi[1:10]

 [1] ".alpha.-(.alpha.-aminopropyl)benzyl alcohol"
 [2] ".alpha.-amylcinnamaldehyde dimethyl acetal" 
 [3] ".alpha.-amylcinnamaldehyde, (E)-"           
 [4] ".alpha.-amylcinnamyl acetate"               
 [5] ".alpha.-chlorohydrin, (+/-)-"               
 [6] ".alpha.-citronellal"                        
 [7] ".alpha.-cubebene, (-)-"                     
 [8] ".alpha.-isomethionyl acetate, (2R)-(+)"     
 [9] ".alpha.-phellandrene"                       
[10] ".alpha.-terpinyl acetate, (+/-)-"           



Create a codelist using drug ingredients:




acetaminophen_codes <- getDrugIngredientCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  name = c("acetaminophen")
)

acetaminophen_codes


- 161_acetaminophen (22708 codes)

section: 2.2. Your turn! Explore the arguments of getDrugIngredientCodes()
text:
2.2. Your turn! Explore the arguments of getDrugIngredientCodes()

We will not explore all the arguments that getDrugIngredientCodes() has. All the information can be found in the package website. Follow the next instructions to create the candidate codelist:
Get drug ingredient codes for the ingredient ‘codeine’ and ‘metformin’
Change the nameStyle of the list to only the concept_name
Restrict to doseForm to “Oral Capsule”.
Restrict to routeCategory “oral”.
Restrict to ingredientRange to only 1 ingredient
Generate a “codelist_with_details”.



Don’t go to the next slide yet… there are the solutions there!

section: 2.2. Your turn! Explore the arguments of getDrugIngredientCodes()
text:
2.2. Your turn! Explore the arguments of getDrugIngredientCodes()

codes <- getDrugIngredientCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  name = c("codeine", "metformin"),
  nameStyle = "{concept_name}",
  doseForm = "Oral Capsule",
  routeCategory = "oral",
  ingredientRange = c(1,1),
  type = "codelist_with_details"
)

codes


- codeine (94 codes)
- metformin (32 codes)

2.3. Exploring codelist

CodelistGenerator has a set of functions to explore what there is in your codelist: the associated* family functions:


associatedConceptClassIds()
associatedDomains()
associatedDoseForms()
associatedDoseUnits()
associatedDrugIngredients()
associatedRouteCategories()
associatedRelationshipIds()
associatedVocabularies()

2.4. Manipulating your codelist: Stratifying

CodelistGenerator has a set of functions to stratify your codelist: the stratify* family functions:


stratifyByRouteCategory()
stratifyByDoseUnit()
stratifyByDomain()
stratifyByVocabulary()
stratifyByDoseForm()
stratifyByConcept()
stratifyByBrand()

2.4. Manipulating your codelist: Subsetting

CodelistGenerator has a set of functions to subset your codelist: the subset* family functions:


subsetToCodesInUse()
subsetOnRouteCategory()
subsetOnDoseUnit()
subsetOnDomain()
subsetOnVocabulary()
subsetOnIngredientRange()
subsetOnDoseForm()

section: 2.4. Manipulating your codelist: Adding or excluding specific concepts
text:
2.4. Manipulating your codelist: Adding or excluding specific concepts

CodelistGenerator has a set of functions to add or exclude specific concepts:


addConcepts()
excludeConcepts()

section: 2.5. Your turn! Let’s manipulate the codelist
text:
2.5. Your turn! Let’s manipulate the codelist

Create a codelist using getDrugIngredientCodes of the ingredient diclophenac, with nameStyle concept_name, and type “codelist_with_details”
Explore which are the associated domains of the codelist
Subset to domain “Drug”
Explore which route categories are available
Subset to all route categories except “transdermal”. Clue: Use the argument negate
Stratify by dose form, keeping the original codelist.
Exclude the concept_id “35873086” from the codelist “diclofenac_dry_powder_inhaler”



Don’t go to the next slide yet… there are the solutions there!

2.5. Solution


codes <- getDrugIngredientCodes(
  cdm = cdm,
  name = c("diclofenac"),
  nameStyle = "{concept_name}",
  type = "codelist_with_details"
)

associatedDomains(codes, cdm)

$diclofenac
[1] "Drug"

codes <- subsetOnDomain(codes, cdm, domain = "Drug")

associatedRouteCategories(codes, cdm)

$diclofenac
[1] "inhalable"                   "injectable"                 
[3] "oral"                        "topical"                    
[5] "transdermal"                 "transmucosal_rectal"        
[7] "unclassified_route_category"

codes <- subsetOnRouteCategory(x = codes, cdm, routeCategory = "transdermal", negate = TRUE)

codes <- stratifyByDoseForm(codes, cdm, keepOriginal = TRUE)

codes <- excludeConcepts(codes, cdm, concepts = 35873086, codelistName = "diclofenac_dry_powder_inhaler")

codes


- diclofenac (11834 codes)
- diclofenac_delayed_release_oral_capsule (153 codes)
- diclofenac_delayed_release_oral_tablet (1606 codes)
- diclofenac_disintegrating_oral_tablet (102 codes)
- diclofenac_effervescent_oral_tablet (8 codes)
- diclofenac_enema (8 codes)
along with 28 more codelists with details

section: Assessing the use of the codes of your codelist: CodelistDiagnostics
text: Assessing the use of the codes of your codelist: CodelistDiagnostics

CodelistDiagnostics









 




summariseAchillesCodeUse Summarises the code use using ACHILLES tables.













 




summariseCodeUse Summarises the code use in patient-level data.

















 




summariseCohortCodeUse Evaluates codelist usage within a cohort.













 




summariseOrphanCodes Identifies orphan codes related to a codelist using ACHILLES tables.

CodelistDiagnostics

Let’s create a codelist:



# Create a codelist for depression
depression <- getCandidateCodes(cdm,
                                keywords = "depression")
depression <- asCodelist(depression)
depression


- candidate_codes (171 codes)

names(depression) <- "depression"
depression


- depression (171 codes)

CodelistDiagnostics: summariseAchillesCodeUse()

ACHILLES is an R package that provides descriptive statistics of an OMOP database.
In other words: when executed, it creates three tables with different counts.
CodelistDiagnostics: summariseAchillesCodeUse()
CodelistDiagnostics: summariseAchillesCodeUse()

CodelistDiagnostics: summariseAchillesCodeUse()

SummariseAchillesCodeUse() uses these tables to calculate the number of people that there are in the database which each one of the concepts present in your codelist.



result <- summariseAchillesCodeUse(depression,
                                   cdm = cdm,
                                   countBy = c("record", "person"))

result

CodelistDiagnostics: summariseCodeUse()

Similarly, summariseCodeUse(), instead of using achilles tables, it calculates using the patient-level data the previous statistics
Pros: you don’t need the achilles tables, you can calculate the counts with more granularity
Cons: computationally expensive

CodelistDiagnostics: summariseCodeUse()

result <- summariseCodeUse(
  depression,
  cdm,
  countBy = c("record", "person"),
  byConcept = TRUE,
  byYear = FALSE,
  bySex = FALSE,
  ageGroup = NULL,
  dateRange = as.Date(c(NA, NA)),
  useSourceCodes = FALSE
)

tableCodeUse(result)

CodelistDiagnostics: summariseCohortCodeUse()

summariseCohortCodeUse() also calculates the number of people that has a specific code within a cohort.
More details after CohortConstructor training another day…

CodelistDiagnostics: summariseOrphanCodes()

Orphan codes are concepts that might be related to codes in your codelist but that have not been included in your codelist.
summariseOrphanCodes() can be used to ensure you are not missing any important concepts.
It also uses ACHILLES tables.



result <- summariseOrphanCodes(depression, cdm)

tableOrphanCodes(result)
